---
title: "About WGRA Notes"
---

**WGRA** ("Wiki-Grounded Research Agent") is a pattern for research-group memory: an LLM-authored wiki that sits between a group's raw sources and its AI coding agent (Claude Code, Cursor, and the like), acting as durable, shared, git-versioned project memory. It is built on the [`llm-wiki-memory-template`](https://github.com/crcresearch/llm-wiki-memory-template).

These **Notes** are the dated companion to the [main WGRA site](https://la3d.github.io/WGRA/). The main site carries the evergreen explainers and case studies; the Notes carry the field record: experiment results, design decisions, and lessons drawn from the project's own working log, plus longer essays on arcs the deep dives do not yet cover.

Two genres:

- **Field notes** turn the wiki's internal log outward, one dated post at a time.
- **Essays** develop the **Research Agents** strategy and its **retrieval foundations**.

Every post declares whether the thing it describes is built, partly built, a design, or a plan, so present tense always means it runs today.

Maintained by Chris Sweet, Center for Research Computing, University of Notre Dame (`csweet1@nd.edu`).
